semanticCore: Fine dust, floating dust, particles in air, vanity, or waste
quranicRelevance: Deeds turned to scattered dust (Haba' - 'haba-an manthooran', 'haba-an munbath-than').

keyDerivations.meaning: Fine floating dust, air particles visible in sunbeams, or empty residue

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَقَدِمْنَا إِلَىٰ مَا عَمِلُوا مِنْ عَمَلٍ فَجَعَلْنَاهُ هَبَاءً مَنْثُورًا (25:23): And We will proceed to what they have done of deeds and make them scattered dust (absolute nullification of polytheistic acts).
summaryEn: The root represents weightless floating dust (Habaa'), serving as an eschatological metaphor for the complete nullification of unguided deeds and the disintegration of mountains.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الغبار الدقيق المتطاير الطافي في الهواء مع أشعة الشمس (هباء)، واصفاً انعدام قيمة أعمال الكفار، وتفتت الجبال هباءً منبثاً.
rawText: هَباَ الرَّمَادُ 1 , (JK, TA,) aor. يَهْبُو, (TA,) The ashes became mixed with dust, and extinguished: (JK, TA:) and هَباَ الجَمْرُ. (M, art. رمد.) هَبَآءٌ The motes that are seen in the rays of the sun: (TA:) see an ex. voce غُثَآءٌ. هَبْوَةٌ Dust rising, or spreading, in the sky like smoke. (JK.) هُبَةٌ : see أَهْبَةٌ.
